rationale: In the citing paper, Goldsmith and Amir (2010) appears only once, in a grouped parenthetical list in the Study 3 discussion. It is used as generic support for a broad claim that prior research distinguishes heuristic versus systematic processing, not as a paper-specific basis for hypotheses, methods, measures, or core interpretation. Because the citation has no distinct role beyond background literature support, this is a mention-only citation.
